analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 counterparty tape study (days 1–3): merge trades to prices at (day,timestamp,symbol); forward mid = K rows ahead in same symbol tape (K∈{5,20,100}); aggressive side vs BBO at print. Outputs in outputs/.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object appended as next JSON element; trader_v0 is no-op until Phase 2 encodes executable rules from ranked edges.

round4_phase1_complete.method_note: Forward horizons are **K tape rows** forward per (day,symbol) after sorting prices by timestamp (not wall-clock ms). Aggressive buy if trade price >= ask1; aggressive sell if price <= bid1; else inside/unknown.

bullet_outputs.conclusion: Strongest pooled cells are **aggressive buys** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** in **tight spread_q=0** (spread ≤ ~25%ile) with **positive same-symbol fwd_5/fwd_20** and high |t| for passive sellers Mark 22 / Mark 49 when Mark 67 is buyer (and Mark 67 as buyer generally); n per cell often 20–45 so interpret cautiously.

bullet_outputs.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pair,symbol,regime cell means)
bullet_outputs.conclusion: Residual CSVs list per-print deviation from (buyer→seller,symbol,spread-regime) mean; use for second-order fades after Phase 2 regularization—not yet converted to PnL.
Mark 01→Mark 22 dominates edge counts (basket ladder); local 500-tick two-trade hop file shows repeated adjacent choreography—structural, not insider-like single names.
Same-(day,timestamp) multi-print bursts show **higher mean forward extract mid** at K=20 vs random single-trade controls (0.33 vs 0.14); K=100 not elevated—short-horizon event, not slow trend.

Ranks passive sellers by mean same-symbol fwd_20 after aggressive buys hit them; use to label who tends to be picked off when lifting offers (Mark 22 on extract appears in high-fwd cells—consistent with informed lift against passive MM).

top5_tradeable_edges.rule: After **multi-symbol burst** timestamps, **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** mid tends **+0.33** by K=20 rows vs **+0.14** control (n≈387 bursts vs 500 controls); K=5 small positive; K=100 flat.
**Mark 67 aggressive buy** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** (price ≥ ask) → same-symbol **fwd_5** positive all three days (day1 mean ~2.20, day2 ~1.75, day3 ~1.93; n≈58/60/45).
top5_tradeable_edges.effect_size: ~+2 mid units at K=5 (same tape indexing)

top5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 01→Mark 22** multi-VEV prints at identical timestamps (initial summary + graph): structural basket; combine with burst forward extract edge for **scheduler** detection.
top5_tradeable_edges.effect_size: 1339+ pair rows (see round4work sketch); not a scalar markout

top5_tradeable_edges.rule: When **Mark 22** is **passive seller** on extract and trade is **aggressive buy**, **fwd_5** cells in **tight book** (spread_q=0) show large positive means (n≈21–26 per session bucket in top-t table).
top5_tradeable_edges.effect_size: mean fwd_5 ~ +2.1 mid units in top cells

top5_tradeable_edges.rule: **Mark 14↔Mark 38** hydro duopoly (from initial summary) — confirm in pair graph; candidate for **fade** or **avoid quoting** on HYDROGEL when opposite side is known aggressor (needs Phase 2 execution).

no_edge_or_weak: Long-horizon **K=100** forward extract after bursts is **not** elevated vs control (mean ~0.12 vs ~0.003)—burst signal is **short** not slow mean-reversion.
Many **VEV** strikes have **thin** aggressive-labeled prints per Mark×day; high |t| cells concentrate on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** where BBO exists at every trade timestamp.
Phase 1 does **not** yet prove **PnL** under `--match-trades worse`; `trader_v0` baseline is zero by design until Phase 2 encodes rules.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2: named-bot burst windows (W=500), microprice vs mid + forward vol proxy on extract, lead-lag extract ret vs Mark01/Mark22 signed flow on VEV_5300, BS IV(5200) means by counterparty pair, Mark01 cumulative net tape series. Backtester sets state.day_num and state.market_trades each tick for R4.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate object follows; trader_v1 tests burst-follow long extract.

bullet_outputs.bullet: 1) Named-bot + burst conditioning (W=500) + trend vs MR proxy
bullet_outputs.conclusion: burst_exact (Mark01 to Mark22, at least 3 symbols same ts) mean fwd_u_20 exceeds isolated trade rows (+0.202 vs +0.143, n=925 vs 3324). burst_near-only slice empty for this merge.

bullet_outputs.bullet: 2) Microstructure (microprice vs mid, forward abs mid sum)
bullet_outputs.conclusion: Extract L1 microprice minus mid plus 5-row forward absolute mid move panel for regime work.
Correlations near zero: signed Mark01 buy minus Mark22 sell flow on VEV_5300 does not linearly lead extract returns at lags 0-5 per day.
Phase 1 spread_q and session strata remain primary; Phase 2 adds burst time axis.
Mean BS IV on VEV_5200 prints highest for Mark38 to Mark22 and Mark14 to Mark22 vs Mark01 to Mark22 (small n for some pairs).
trader_v1 naive long extract after burst: total +7207 worse and all; day3 VELVET -691. Mid edge not robust to naive fills.
Cumulative Mark01 net from tape exported for future skew rules.

top5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Mark01 to Mark22 exact burst timestamps associate with higher mean extract forward +20 rows vs isolated enriched trade rows.
top5_tradeable_edges_phase2.orthogonal_to_p1: Control is isolated trades not random timestamp sample.

top5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Mark38 to Mark22 and Mark14 to Mark22 show higher mean IV on VEV_5200 prints than Mark01 to Mark22 under same BS.
Microprice minus mid on extract with forward abs mid sum5 for regime gating (stack with Sonic in Phase 3).
Runtime burst detection via state.market_trades matches offline Mark01 to Mark22 multi-symbol pattern.
Negative: linear lead-lag from VEV_5300 signed flow to extract returns is absent at lags 0-5.

round4_phase2_complete.interaction_with_phase1: Phase 1 burst vs random control showed large extract forward lift; Phase 2 burst_exact vs isolated trade rows confirms direction with smaller gap. trader_v1 shows PnL positive on days 1-2 but day 3 loss: refines Phase 1 that tape edge requires execution gating (Phase 3 Sonic joint gate).

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 3: Sonic joint gate (VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 spread both <=2) on R4 prices; inclineGod spread-spread and spread-extract correlations; three-way burst x tight x fwd extract +20; trader_v2 stacks gate on burst-follow.
used_to_decide: round4_phase3_complete object follows; compare trader_v1 vs trader_v2 PnL for gate interaction with naive execution.

round4_phase3_complete.phase: Round 4 Phase 3 (Sonic joint gate + counterparty synthesis)
round4_phase3_complete.sonic_gate_definition: Same as R3 script: inner join VEV_5200, VEV_5300, VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT on (day,timestamp); spread = ask1-bid1; tight_joint = (s5200<=2) and (s5300<=2). Merged onto enriched trade rows by (day,timestamp).
key_findings: On enriched trades, **non-burst** rows with **tight** joint gate show **much higher** mean extract fwd_20 (+0.456, n=1217) than **burst+tight** (+0.202, n=925) or **non-tight** (-0.038, n=2107). So the Sonic surface state dominates the simple mid-forward signal in this slice; Mark01->Mark22 bursts in R4 often occur when the gate is already tight.
**Mark01->Mark22** prints on **any** symbol: almost all at tight=True (1324 vs 3 at tight=False); mean fwd_u20 is modest (+0.254) when tight — pair identity alone does not beat the gate.

**Sim PnL:** `trader_v2` (burst + require tight to quote) total **+1766** worse vs `trader_v1` (burst only) **+7207** on same days — **stricter gate reduces fills and total PnL** for this naive long rule, even though day 3 is no longer negative on extract in v2.

tier_a_edges_ranked.statement: Largest mean forward extract mid among three-way cells on trade-timestamp merge.
Counterparty pair is almost always observed under tight gate; forward mean positive but smaller than non-burst tight.
corr(s5200,s5300) ~0.18-0.27 by day; corr spreads vs extract mid positive ~0.32-0.47 on aligned panel.

tier_a_edges_ranked.interaction: (abs extract mid change vs abs spread changes)
tier_a_edges_ranked.statement: Weak positive corr between |dm_ext| and |ds5200|, stronger for |ds5300| on day 3.
All Mark01->Mark22 multi-VEV bursts in sample align with tight timestamps (no burst+tight=False rows); burst+tight mean fwd_u20 +0.202 matches Phase 2 burst_exact isolated comparison.

round4_phase3_complete.comparison_without_gate: Phase 1/2 pooled forward extract after bursts did not condition on 5200/5300. Phase 3 shows **tight alone** already implies large positive fwd_u20 on trade timestamps; **adding burst on top of tight lowers** the mean vs **tight without burst** in this merge. Sonic gate **does not increase** the naive burst-follow sim PnL (v2 << v1); it **filters** to safer but **smaller** opportunity set.
round4_phase3_complete.interaction_phase1_phase2: Phase 1 burst vs control and Phase 2 burst_exact vs isolated both highlighted burst extract lift. Phase 3 refines: **relative to other tight-timestamp trades**, burst rows are **not** the highest forward mean — the gate **cleans** the population (tight vs wide) as Sonic suggests, but **Mark01->Mark22 burst is not the unique driver** of positive fwd_u20 once tight is controlled.
round4_phase3_complete.followup_day_stability_extract_fwd20: Recomputed three-way means using **always VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** mid +20 rows at each trade timestamp (`r4_p3_threeway_extract_fwd20_by_day.csv`). **nb_tight** is positive all days (0.63, 0.72, 0.25). **b_tight** flips sign across days (-0.47, -0.18, +0.97) — **not stable** for burst+tight as a simple long trigger; **nb_tight** is the more consistent mid-only signal.

analysis_description: Post-Phase-3: added `r4_p3_threeway_extract_fwd20_by_day.csv` to phase3 script (extract forward always). Implemented `trader_v3.py` — Sonic tight joint gate only, two-sided MM on VELVET + VEV_5100-5500; wide regime small trim. Backtest vs v1/v2.
used_to_decide: v3 worse +4672 < v1 +7207 but > v2 +1766; naive tight-MM still loses on VEV wings day3. Next: inventory skew or avoid quoting when Mark22 sells at touch (Phase 1 adverse table).

analysis_description: Counterparty-conditioned execution on top of Phase 3 tight MM: offline counts of VELVET aggressive buys (price>=ask1) with passive seller Mark22/Mark49 per day; `trader_v4` skips extract sell quotes when same pattern appears in `state.market_trades` at the tick.
used_to_decide: v4 worse +6793 improves v3 +4672 by +2121; extract PnL +2170 vs +1018 v3. Still below v1 burst-long +7207. Trigger rate ~53-71 aggressive Mark22/49 sells per day on extract tape.

analysis_description: Mirror adverse selection: from enriched trades, aggressive **sell** on VELVET with passive **buyer** Mark49 has mean fwd20 ~ -2.73 (n=15). Built `r4_p5_adverse_aggrsell_fwd20_by_passive_buyer.csv` and `r4_v5_m49_aggr_sell_counts.csv`. `trader_v5` = v4 + skip extract **buy** when tape shows aggressive sell through bid with Mark49 passive buyer.
used_to_decide: **Negative ablation:** v5 worse +6305 < v4 +6793; symmetric Mark49 buy-skip hurts MM under worse fills despite negative tape fwd20 for that cell (small n=15; inventory/asymmetry dominates). Keep v4 as better counterparty-augmented tight-MM.
